409 Kb - - tor,freebsd,christian bruffer,avi - - - - - - Manuel Trujillo - FreeBSD para usuarios de GNU/Linux - - Charla sobre las diferencias que puede encontrar un usuario - de un sistema operativo GNU/Linux cuando accede a un sistema - operativo FreeBSD, y sugerencias superar la posible - desorientación. -

- ]]> - http://bcn.bsdcon.net/presentations.html - bsdcon-barcelona,spanish,presentation,freebsd,linux,manuel trujillo - - - http://bcn.bsdcon.net/presentaciones/FreeBSD_para_linuxeros.ppt - 32 Kb - pdf - - - - - - Jordi Prats - Uso de OpenBSD en dispositivos empotrados - - Los sistemas empotrados gracias a un menor consumo - energético y unas dimensiones reducidas, a costa - de ciertas limitaciones del hardware, permiten su uso - en multitud de entornos. En esta presentación - veremos como usarlos con OpenBSD y sus posibles aplicaciones. -

- ]]> - http://bcn.bsdcon.net/presentations.html - bsdcon-barcelona,spanish,presentation,openbsd,embedded,jordi prats - - - http://bcn.bsdcon.net/presentaciones/embeddedBSD.pdf - 1.8 Mb - 44 pages - pdf - - - - - - <![CDATA[Jesús Rodriguez - SIP y VozIP con FreeBSD]]> - - Repaso a las diferentes aplicaciones y servicios - relacionados con SIP y VozIP que pueden usarse en - FreeBSD. Entre estas apliaciones destacan OpenSER y - Asterisk, ya que usados de forma conjunta pueden ofrecer - una larga lista de servicios de forma rápida, - segura y escalable. -

- ]]> - http://bcn.bsdcon.net/presentations.html - bsdcon-barcelona,spanish,presentation,asterisk,openser,freebsd,sip,voip,jesus rodriguez - - - http://bcn.bsdcon.net/presentaciones/bsdcon-asterisk-openser.pdf - 527 Kb - 40 pages - pdf - - - - - - Jordi Espasa Clofent - Sistema de cortafuegos redundantes con OpenBSD y Packet Filter en modo bridge - - Se trataran los siguientes apartados: Porqué OpenBSD - y porqué PF. Eligiendo un buen hardware para el - cortafuegos. Redundancia en modo bridge: RSTP. - Implementación en si. -

- ]]> - http://bcn.bsdcon.net/presentations.html - bsdcon-barcelona,spanish,presentation,openbsd,firewall,pf,jordi espasa clofent - - - http://bcn.bsdcon.net/presentaciones/sistema_cortafuegos_redundantes_openbsd.ppt - 1 Mb - ppt - - - - - - Julio M. Merino Vidal - ATF: Sistema de pruebas automatizado para NetBSD - - La presentación empezará describiendo la - necesidad de poder probar automáticamente la - validez del código del sistema operativo NetBSD - para así saber que se comporta correctamente en - cualquiera de las plataformas soportadas. Luego se - explicará cómo se estructura ATF, cómo - se integra con NetBSD y se daran ejemplos prácticos - de su uso tanto como programador o usuario. -

- ATF es un proyecto autocontenido que funciona en multitud - de plataformas (y no sólo BSD). Aún así, - está centrado en NetBSD y las pruebas automatizadas - para este sistema son específicas de él, no - del proyecto ATF en sí. -

- ]]> - http://bcn.bsdcon.net/presentations.html - bsdcon-barcelona,spanish,presentation,atf,netbsd,julio m merino vidal - - - http://bcn.bsdcon.net/presentaciones/ATF.pptx - 234 Kb - ppt - - - - - - Robert Watson - How a large scale opensource project works - - The FreeBSD Project is one of the oldest and most - successful open source operating system projects, - seeing wide deployment across the IT industry. From - the root name servers, to top tier ISPs, to core - router operating systems, to firewalls, to embedded - appliances, you can't use a networked computer for - ten minutes without using FreeBSD dozens of times. -

- Part of FreeBSD's reputation for quality and - reliability comes from the nature of its development - organization -- driven by a hundreds of highly - skilled volunteers, from high school students to - university professors. And unlike most open source - projects, the FreeBSD Project has developers who - have been working on the same source base for over - twenty years. -

- But how does this organization work? Who pays the - bandwidth bills, runs the web servers, writes the - documentation, writes the code, and calls the shots? - And how can developers in a dozen time zones reach - agreement on the time of day, let alone a kernel - architecture? -

- This presentation will attempt to provide, in 45 - minutes, a brief if entertaining snapshot into what - makes FreeBSD run. -

- ]]> - http://fosdem.org/2008/schedule/events/large_scale_opensource - fosdem,fosdem2008,presentation,freebsd project,robert watson - - - http://video.fosdem.org/2008/maintracks/FOSDEM2008-largescale.ogg - 81 Mb - 45 minutes - ogg,video - - - - @@ -9749,131 +9315,6 @@ berklix,netbsd,packages - - - Interview with Will Backman - http://www.linuxreality.com/podcast/episode-74-interview-with-will-backman/ - - In this episode: an interview with the host of the - BSDTalk Podcast, Will Backman, in which we talk - about the history of the BSD's, including FreeBSD, - NetBSD, OpenBSD, DragonflyBSD, PC-BSD, and DesktopBSD, - and discuss some of the goals and features of these - projects. - - linux reality,bsdtalk,interview,will backman - - - http://media.libsyn.com/media/linuxreality/linuxreality074.mp3 - 48 minutes - 21 Mb - MP3 file - mp3 - - - - - BSD Wrap-Up - http://www.linuxreality.com/podcast/episode-75-bsd-wrap-up/ - - In this episode: OReilly discount code for Linux - Reality listeners available on the LR website; a - new Linux Reality contest where one can win a - listener-donated book, LPI Certification in a - Nutshell, for the best audio Listener Tip sent in - between now and the end of November; a new podcast - client I am developing in Python; petition to open - source the Main Actor video editing software; a - call for guest podcasts; a brief wrap-up discussion - of my adventures with the BSD's; audio and email - listener feedback. - - linux reality - - - http://media.libsyn.com/media/linuxreality/linuxreality075.mp3 - 48 minutes - 21 Mb - MP3 file - mp3 - - - - - - - - OpenBSD Road Warrior - Felix Kronlage - http://www.sitescollide.com/Podcast/CD527090-CAAC-4BEC-ACC1-2E3FC930DC80.html - - On this Sites Collide, we speak with Felix Kronlage - of the OpenBSD Project about using Open-Source tools - for effectively and securely getting work done while - using your laptop outside your home or office (called - a Road Warrior). If you use a laptop on the road, - you NEED to hear this episode. - - sitescollide,interview,openbsd,road warrior,felix kronlage - - - http://feeds.feedburner.com/~r/SitesCollide/~5/142146005/scr012-1.mp3 - 40 minutes - 16 Mb - MP3 file - mp3 - - - - - - What is OpenBSD - Wim Vandeputte - http://www.sitescollide.com/Podcast/C8F1C6F7-72B1-4E47-B51A-42212989BF19.html - - In this episode of Sites Collide, Wim Vandeputte - of the OpenBSD project joins us to educate us about - OpenBSD. We talk about a brief history, as well as - where you can find it running today. If you don't - know the difference between BSD and Linux, you need - to hear this episode!! - - sitescollide,interview,openbsd,wim vandeputte - - - http://feeds.feedburner.com/~r/SitesCollide/~5/136752314/scr010.mp3 - 46 minutes - 18 Mb - MP3 file - mp3 - - - - - - OpenBSD Networking - Henning Brauer - http://www.sitescollide.com/Podcast/B50EE425-0C8B-4AF2-AD70-CD2C98D091B3.html - - In this episode of Sites Collide, we discuss Open - BGPd and OpenBSD as well as other routing-related - topics with developer, network guru, and conference - speaker, Henning Brauer of the OpenBSD Project. So, - if you are interested in the technologies that make - the Internet work, or you're looking to learn about - Unix/Linux, this show is for you! - - sitescollide,interview,openbsd,openbgpd,henning brauer - - - http://feeds.feedburner.com/~r/SitesCollide/~5/136752318/scr08.mp3 - 20 minutes - 8 Mb - MP3 file - mp3 - - - - @@ -12744,21 +12185,11 @@ http://www.asiabsdcon.org/ - - linuxreality - a podcast for the new linux user - http://www.linuxreality.com/ - - Berklix.com Computer Services http://www.berklix.com/ - - Sites Collide - http://www.sitescollide.com/ - - BSDCan - The Technical BSD Conference http://www.bsdcan.org/ _______________________________________________ svn-doc-all@freebsd.org mailing list http://lists.freebsd.org/mailman/listinfo/svn-doc-all To unsubscribe, send any mail to "svn-doc-all-unsubscribe@freebsd.org"
